verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic/mitochondrial chaperone (member of TIM23 complex, involved in protein folding in the cytosol and organelles) with no transmembrane domain or membrane-anchoring mechanism. However, Hsp70-derived peptides are well-documented MHC-presented antigens recognized by tumor-reactive T cells and are engaged by therapeutic vaccine and TCR-targeting programs, qualifying the protein as clinically accessible via pMHC.
